meta.disclaimer: TSO publications are explicitly non-binding first assessments; coordinates approximate (municipality); MW bands indicative.

sources.title: Reifegradverfahren für Netzanschlüsse an das Übertragungsnetz — Konzept der Übertragungsnetzbetreiber, V1.00, 05.02.2026
Amprion — Potenzielle Netzanschlussmöglichkeiten inkl. ergänzendes Dokument (Stand 04.2026)
50Hertz — Netzkapazität map data feed (75 substations: queue, free bays, BKZ; snapshot in data/grid_reform/raw/50hertz_gridcapacity.json)
TenneT — Netzanschlussanfragen (coordinate table of round-1 locations + Umspannwerks-Projekte map, 04.2026)
Gemeinsame Pressemitteilung der vier ÜNB zum Reifegradverfahren, 05.02.2026

framework.name: Reifegradverfahren
framework.scope: Storage and large consumers (BESS, data centres, electrolysers, industry) seeking a transmission-grid connection; DSO ad-hoc bulk connections ≥100 MW.
framework.legal_basis: KraftNAV amendment in force 2025-12-24 (storage excluded from KraftNAV); BNetzA confirmation foreseen, EnWG amendments proposed.
framework.scarce_resource: Free switch bays (Schaltfelder) in transmission substations — not MW. Nearly all free bays are reserved for the next five years.
framework.three_gw_rule: Design faults may never trip more than 3 GW of generation or load at once; capacity is assigned per site under this criterion.

bkz_tranches.due: 18 months after tranche 1 or start of construction

sub.label: Permitting status (strategy → formally acknowledged by authorities)
Project plan (technical sketch, site layout, schedule)
Customer substation (primary / secondary technology plans)
sub.min_req: VDE form, P(t) ramp-up, single-line diagram

sub.label: Routing of the tie-line (corridor → detail survey → secured easements)
Procurement of long-lead items (inventory → offers → signed purchase contracts)
Project hybridisation (co-location of 2 → 3 technologies → 3 + overbuilt generation)

phases.label: Phase 1 · Info & application
phases.note: Capacity data published; deadline 30.06.2026 24:00 with €50k fee

phases.label: Phase 2 · Cluster study
phases.note: Admissibility → maturity scoring → bay assignment → load-flow study

phases.label: Phase 3 · Offers
phases.note: 1 month to accept by paying €1,500/MW deposit; runner-up round

notes: 50Hertz alone holds ~65 GW of BESS connection requests; capacities for commissioning 2025–2029 are exhausted in its zone.
Reserved BESS capacity (51 GW) already exceeds the lower NEP 2037 scenario need (41 GW).

substations.name: Clusorth
substations.why: Same Emsland logic as Meppen: wind-saturated corridor — only load connections reduce congestion, hence load-only from 2035.

substations.name: Esch
substations.why: Rhineland lignite district: Niederaußem/Neurath retirements free bays and headroom at the Cologne ring; NEP keeps Esch a major node.

nep.note: mentions rise 5→12→25 across NEPs — increasingly central to the post-lignite Rhineland grid.

substations.name: Flör
substations.why: Westmünsterland / NL interconnection area; capacity follows NEP reinforcement of the border corridor.

nep.note: Hanekenfähr–Gronau (BBP 63) with a phase-shifter AT Flör — that is the enabler of the 2032 offer.

substations.name: Herringhausen
substations.why: Feed-in only at reduced power until upstream NEP reinforcements complete (~2035).

substations.name: Kötz
substations.why: Bavarian Swabia node; bays realisable but feed-in reduced until the regional 380-kV upgrades land.

substations.name: Kühmoos
substations.why: High Rhine / Swiss border, pumped-storage corridor; capacity appears with the Hochrhein reinforcement. Shared node with TransnetBW.

substations.name: Rieselfeld
substations.why: Ruhr region: coal phase-out frees grid headroom, but feed-in reduced until line upgrades; load welcome.

substations.name: Meppen
substations.why: Emsland/NL-border corridor is feed-in-saturated; load here is grid-serving (absorbs northern wind surplus), so bays are earmarked for consumers only.

substations.name: Pöppinghausen
substations.why: Ruhr coal phase-out frees bays; 2033 after switchgear extension.

substations.name: Wittenhorst
substations.why: Lower Rhine near the Doetinchem–Wesel NL interconnector; NEP corridor strengthening enables bays from 2033.

nep.note: new switchgear listed in the Punktmaßnahmen registry.

substations.name: Altbach
substations.why: Coal→gas fuel-switch site; connection only temporary until 2045 because the node is re-planned in the NEP.

substations.name: Birkenfeld
substations.why: Node west of Pforzheim strengthened in the NEP; both n-0 and n-1 bays realisable from 2032.

nep.note: was in NEP 2019–2023 (5 mentions each), dropped out of NEP 2025 — reinforcement done/committed.

substations.name: Bühl
substations.why: Same Rheinschiene reinforcement as Kork; big feed-in band, small load band.

substations.name: Eichstetten
substations.why: Endpoint of the new Daxlanden–Eichstetten Rheinschiene line; biggest band in BW (1.5–2 GW) but temporary until 2045 — the node is re-dedicated afterwards.

substations.name: Gurtweil
substations.why: High Rhine / Swiss border hydro corridor; Hochrhein upgrade brings capacity 2032.

substations.name: Kork
substations.why: Upper Rhine valley; capacity rides on the NEP Badische Rheinschiene 380-kV reinforcement (2032).

substations.name: Philippsburg
substations.why: Nuclear site (KKP off 2011/2019): the plant's bays and strong lines remain, and the Ultranet HVDC converter makes it a premium node — capacity from 2032.

substations.name: Schwörstadt
substations.why: Small High-Rhine node on the same corridor, 2032.

substations.name: Mannheim
substations.why: GKM hard-coal retirement frees capacity; TSO explicitly bars BESS here ("keine BESS") — plausibly short-circuit-power and stability duties of the node, reason not published.

substations.name: Daxlanden
substations.why: RDK power-plant site Karlsruhe: 0 MW feed-in (already used), but 0.5–1 GW load headroom from 2035.

substations.name: Engslatt
substations.why: Balingen area, long-term after regional 380-kV upgrades (2035).

substations.name: Kühmoos (TNG)
substations.why: TransnetBW side of the shared Kühmoos node; long-term 2035.

substations.name: Mannheim Ost
substations.why: Second Mannheim node, long-term (2035) once GKM exit and regional reinforcements settle.

substations.name: Pfullendorf
substations.why: Oberschwaben node, long-term 2035, small bands.

substations.name: Wendlingen
substations.why: Neckar valley node, long-term 2035 (nearby Altbach/Bünzwangen occupied).

substations.name: Cappeln West
substations.why: Brand-new NEP substation (BImSchG-approved, under construction) in the Cloppenburg wind belt; built to collect onshore wind — customer bays from 2030.

nep.note: in the plan since NEP 2021 (Cloppenburg wind build-out).

substations.name: Garrel Ost
substations.why: Twin of Cappeln West, 25 km apart; same new-build logic, 2030.

substations.name: Göhl West
substations.why: Ostholstein endpoint of the new Ostküstenleitung; wind-saturated coast → load-only offer.

substations.name: Großenmeer
substations.why: Wesermarsch node on the Conneforde corridor collecting coastal and offshore wind; 2031.

substations.name: Hochwöhrden
substations.why: Dithmarschen / Westküstenleitung corridor; wind-saturated, electrolysis target region (Heide) → load-only.

nep.note: appears only in NEP 2025 (reactive-power compensation program); offer is Westküste wind-saturation logic, load-only.

substations.name: Kiel Rönne
substations.why: The new Kiel 380-kV substation from the NEP (Audorf–Kiel–Göhl ring); bays come with line completion ~2036.

substations.name: Neuenkirchen
substations.why: Same Conneforde/Wesermarsch build-out, 2031.

substations.name: Pleinting
substations.why: Site of the retired Pleinting oil plant on the Danube — legacy strong node reactivated after reinforcement, 2034.

substations.name: Sande
substations.why: Near Wilhelmshaven: offshore landing point and planned H2/industry hub; 2033.

substations.name: Wenzenbach
substations.why: Near Regensburg, endpoint of the Ostbayernring reinforcement; bays with completion ~2036.

substations.name: Graustein (GRS)
substations.why: 1950s lignite-era node in Lusatia (built for the Boxberg/Lübbenau complex): coal decline frees 5 bays. Feed-in is already RE-saturated (0 MW), but 1.9 GW load headroom.

substations.name: Hamburg/Nord (NOR)
substations.why: Hamburg ring reinforcement; 3 bays and 700 MW kept free — city load growth expected.

substations.name: Hohenwarte II (HOH2) Schaltanlage
substations.why: Pumped-storage plant switchyard (220 kV): plant-scale bays exist, so ~2 GW is technically available.

substations.name: Jessen/Nord (JSN)
substations.why: Wind-collection node in Saxony-Anhalt; bays built with the RE build-out, 1.5–1.9 GW available.

substations.name: Neuenhagen (NHG)
substations.why: Berlin ring node (110 kV listing); 4 bays with modest available capacity.

substations.name: Ragow (RAG)
substations.why: Lusatia node next to the former Lübbenau plant site; lignite exit frees bays.

substations.name: Siedenbrünzow (SOW)
substations.why: Mecklenburg wind-collection node; 1 bay free, 0.5/0.9 GW still available.

nep.note: corridor rebuild brings the new bays.

substations.name: Streumen (STR)
substations.why: Saxony node; 1 bay, 200 MW available.

substations.name: Altentreptow Süd (ATS)
substations.why: Mecklenburg wind belt: bays exist from RE build-out, but 5.2 GW requested vs 0.65 GW available — Germany's most oversubscribed node.

substations.name: Eulenberg (EUB) Neubau
substations.why: Brand-new 50Hertz substation near Magdeburg/A14, designed 400→1,600 MW — new bays, top BKZ (100 €/kW).

substations.name: Gransee (GSE)
substations.why: Rural Brandenburg node recently extended for wind collection; 1.55 GW still available.

substations.name: Heinersdorf (HND)
substations.why: East-Brandenburg node; 1 bay, 0.5/0.7 GW available.

nep.note: 380-kV Kabeldiagonale Berlin — city cable rebuild not done before 2038.

substations.name: Eulenberg (EUB)
substations.why: Brand-new 50Hertz substation near Magdeburg/A14 (industrial build-out corridor), designed 400→1,600 MW — hence 1.8–2 GW still available but BKZ 100 €/kW.

substations.name: Iven/West (IVW)
substations.why: Mecklenburg node; 1 bay free, load headroom 1.35 GW.

analysis_overview.text: Grid planned around the coal phase-out and 65% renewables. Large batteries barely exist in the scenarios (low single-digit GW), and connection bays are sized for known projects only. None of today's queue was anticipated.

First climate-neutrality plan: ~70 GW offshore, four new HVDC corridors, and a wave of brand-new 380-kV substations — including the TenneT sites now offered for 2030–2036 (Ostküstenleitung, Cloppenburg wind belt, Wesermarsch). Their bays only arrive with the lines.

First plan that carries the storage wave (41–94 GW BESS by 2037) and data-centre load. But its new bays land 2030+, while 211 GW of BESS requests arrived 2023–2025 — the mismatch that forced the Reifegradverfahren.

Capacity exists where conventional plants died (Philippsburg nuclear, Mannheim/Altbach/Ruhr coal, Lusatia lignite — bays freed), where NEP new-builds land (TenneT 2030–36 sites, Eulenberg), and where load is grid-serving in wind-saturated areas (load-only offers at Göhl, Hochwöhrden, Clusorth, Meppen).

Machine-parsing all four NEP Projektsteckbriefe shows two offer classes. NEP-driven: the offer year equals a project COD (Großenmeer = TTG-P119 2031, Kühmoos = AMP-P204 2035, Rheinschiene nodes = TNG-P49 2030, Kiel Rönne = TTG-P71 2037, Flör = a new phase-shifter in BBPlG 63). Legacy: Clusorth, Kötz, Rieselfeld, Kork, Engslatt, Schwörstadt, Altbach and others appear in NO NEP — those offers ride on existing bays and assets, and are gone once taken.

analysis_compare.title: Philippsburg (offered) vs Karlsruhe-West/Ost (red)
analysis_compare.text: Philippsburg inherited a nuclear plant's oversized switchyard and gets the Ultranet converter (~2027) on top. The Karlsruhe nodes feed the city and the RDK site — their bays are consumed by DSO transformers and industry, and there is no room to extend inside the urban footprint.

analysis_compare.title: Graustein, 5 free bays vs Röhrsdorf/Zwönitz, none
analysis_compare.text: Lignite-era switchyards were built oversized for power plants that are now gone — Graustein (Boxberg complex) keeps plant-scale bays. Ordinary regional supply nodes were sized to demand and have nothing spare.

analysis_compare.title: Siedenbrünzow & Iven/West (bays free) vs Pasewalk (0)
analysis_compare.text: All three sit on the BBPlG-53 corridor. Siedenbrünzow and Iven/West are being rebuilt with new switchgear as part of the line project; at Pasewalk 1.1 GW is already reserved and the remaining bays are committed.

analysis_compare.title: Altentreptow Süd (1 bay) vs Altentreptow Nord (0)
analysis_compare.text: Süd was extended during the wind build-out and kept a spare bay; Nord's diameter is fully occupied. Same town, opposite queue position — bays, not MW, decide.

analysis_compare.title: TransnetBW green vs red
analysis_compare.text: The 9 green nodes sit on corridors the NEP is reinforcing anyway (Rheinschiene, Hochrhein) or on retired-plant sites (Philippsburg, Mannheim, Altbach) with physical room for new bays. The 37 red ones are mostly urban/DSO supply nodes — every bay feeds a 110-kV transformer already.

analysis_compare.title: Load-only offers (Göhl, Hochwöhrden, Meppen, Clusorth)
analysis_compare.text: In wind-saturated coastal areas new feed-in would deepen congestion, but new load absorbs surplus and cuts redispatch — so the TSO offers the bay, but only for consumers.

candidates.name: Sommerland
candidates.driver: New UW in Gemeinde Sommerland (Kreis Steinburg), looped into both 380-kV circuits — upsized from 2 to 4 380/110-kV transformers between NEP 2023 and NEP 2025 (TTG-P24/P26 Unterelbe corridor)
candidates.effect: A doubling between drafts is the plan reacting to demand: prime vertical load-injection node between Hamburg and the west coast.

candidates.name: Leingarten (Großgartach)
candidates.driver: SuedLink southern converter (BBPlG 3), COD 2028
candidates.effect: Highest-confidence red→green flip: Großgartach is 'nicht verfügbar' today only because the converter is being built. 2 GW DC lands here in 2028.

candidates.name: Bommersheim
candidates.driver: TTG-P486 Großkrotzenburg/West – Frankfurt/Nord – Suchraum Bommersheim, COD 2032
candidates.effect: New node in the Frankfurt data-centre belt — will be the most oversubscribed offer of cycle 2 the moment it appears. Load-only.

candidates.name: Frankfurt/Nord
candidates.driver: Same TTG-P486 build-out, plus AMP-P161 (southeast of Frankfurt, COD 2030) and P47 Frankfurt–Karlsruhe (2031)
candidates.effect: Three projects converge on Rhine-Main 2030–32; structural answer to the region's load queue.

candidates.name: Rheinau (Mannheim)
candidates.driver: AMP-P309 Biblis–Bürstadt–Rheinau–Hoheneck (ad-hoc), COD 2031
candidates.effect: Red today; the corridor rebuild through it is the classic Iven/West pattern — expect a flip with the 2031 COD.

candidates.name: Karlsruhe-West
candidates.driver: TNG-P49 Rheinschiene (COD 2030) + P47 Frankfurt–Karlsruhe (2031) pass directly by
candidates.effect: Red today (urban bays consumed); at least one Karlsruhe-area node should open when the corridors energise. Uncertain which.

candidates.name: Güstrow (completion)
candidates.driver: Hub of three corridor rebuilds: P34 Güstrow–Wolmirstedt (2028), P215 Güstrow–Bentwisch–Gnewitz (2029), P216 Güstrow–Pasewalk (2031)
candidates.effect: 2 GW requested today against nothing free; the triple rebuild is what changes that — the Iven/West pattern at three lines at once.

candidates.name: Rostock ring (Bentwisch · Gnewitz · Lüdershagen)
candidates.driver: 50HzT-P215 (BBPlG 52), COD 2029; Gnewitz already has a new 380-kV switchyard in the Punktmaßnahmen registry
candidates.effect: New bays along the rebuilt Rostock ring from 2029 — earliest corridor completion in the northeast.

candidates.name: Mitte/Reinickendorf (Berlin)
candidates.driver: 50HzT-P531 Berlin build-out (BBPlG 87), first sections ~2029
candidates.effect: With the Kabeldiagonale not done before 2038, this and Großbeeren are Berlin's only plausible openings before the mid-2030s.

candidates.name: Unterelbe (Büttel/Süd · Wilster/West · Stade/West)
candidates.driver: TTG-P25/P26 NordElbe (BBPlG 50) + Stade/West new-build under TTG-P24, COD ~2031
candidates.effect: Industrial Unterelbe cluster (Brunsbüttel–Stade chemistry, LNG, H2): new switchgear at three nodes.

candidates.name: Pöschendorf
candidates.driver: 37 mentions in NEP 2025 (new SH corridor node, Kreis Steinburg)
candidates.effect: The biggest unoffered new node in Schleswig-Holstein; wind-saturated region → expect load-only.

candidates.name: Stegau / Albersdorf
candidates.driver: TTG-P476 Netzausbau Hochwöhrden–Stegau, COD 2032; new switchgear at both (Punktmaßnahmen)
candidates.effect: Extends the Westküste/Heide H2 logic east — sister offers to Hochwöhrden, load-only.

candidates.name: Melbeck
candidates.driver: Ostniedersachsenleitung (TTG-P113), COD 2032; new 380-kV switchgear (Punktmaßnahme M777SA1)
candidates.effect: New node south of Lüneburg on the SH–Niedersachsen axis.

candidates.name: Pirach
candidates.driver: TTG-P112 Pirach–Pleinting–St. Peter, COD 2030; 3×380/110-kV transformers planned
candidates.effect: Bavarian chemical triangle (Burghausen) load node — pairs with the already-offered Pleinting.

candidates.name: Oberbachern – Ottenhofen
candidates.driver: TTG-P222 reinforcement north of Munich, COD 2030
candidates.effect: Munich-region load has nowhere else to connect; watch both endpoints.

candidates.name: Osterath
candidates.driver: A-Nord + Ultranet DC hub (BBPlG 1/2)
candidates.effect: 4 GW of HVDC lands here; converter build-out adds bays at the Rhineland's strongest node — watch for load offers after COD.

candidates.name: Emden Ost
candidates.driver: A-Nord northern converter (BBPlG 1)
candidates.effect: Wind-saturated coast: expect load-only offers (electrolysis) once the DC line exports the surplus.

candidates.name: Brunsbüttel
candidates.driver: SuedLink northern converter at the ex-nuclear site (BBPlG 3/4)
candidates.effect: Converter + freed plant bays + industrial site: strong candidate for large load in Schleswig-Holstein.

candidates.name: Bergrheinfeld West
candidates.driver: SuedLink Bavarian converter (BBPlG 4)
candidates.effect: 2 GW DC into Franconia; candidate for load/BESS offers around Schweinfurt after COD.

candidates.name: Wolmirstedt
candidates.driver: SuedOstLink converter (BBPlG 5) + P124 corridor COD 2027
candidates.effect: Published today with 0 free bays (750 MW reserved); the converter build-out adds switchgear — expect new offers after 2028.

candidates.name: Isar
candidates.driver: SuedOstLink southern converter at the ex-nuclear site (BBPlG 5)
candidates.effect: 2 GW DC + freed nuclear bays: prime candidate for Bavarian load/BESS.

candidates.name: Querfurt
candidates.driver: New 380-kV switchgear on the Wolmirstedt–Lauchstädt axis (50HzT-P124, COD 2027; Punktmaßnahmen registry)
candidates.effect: Brand-new node in the central-German chemical-triangle load region — one of the earliest new bays anywhere.

candidates.name: Großbeeren
candidates.driver: Berlin build-out 50HzT-P531 (BBPlG 87): Thyrow–Großbeeren–Berlin/Südost + new switchgear/transformers
candidates.effect: South-Berlin logistics/data-centre belt gets its own 380-kV injection; NEP mentions jumped 2→21 between 2023 and 2025.

candidates.name: Suchraum Dresden-Nord
candidates.driver: New 380-kV switchgear + 3×380/110-kV transformers, Suchraum Moritzburg/Radeburg/Ottendorf-Okrilla (50HzT-P364, first NEP 2023)
candidates.effect: Purpose-built vertical injection for the Dresden-North / Silicon-Saxony chip belt — a future large-load connection point.

candidates.name: Werderland / Bötersen
candidates.driver: TTG-P119 (BBPlG 56) Conneforde–Elsfleth/West–Bötersen: two new 380-kV switchgear sites
candidates.effect: Brand-new Bremen-region nodes on the same 2031 line that makes the Großenmeer offer possible.

candidates.name: Sengwarden
candidates.driver: TTG-P175 (BBPlG 73) Wilhelmshaven–Conneforde, new switchgear
candidates.effect: Wilhelmshaven energy-hub sister node to the offered Sande — COD already 2027.

candidates.name: Güstrow–Pasewalk corridor
candidates.driver: 50HzT-P216 (BBPlG 53) Güstrow–Siedenbrünzow–Iven/West–Pasewalk, COD 2031
candidates.effect: More bays and rising available MW at Pasewalk/Nord and Pasewalk as the rebuild that created the Iven/West offer completes.

candidates.name: Ulzburg
candidates.driver: Ostküstenleitung start point (BBPlG 42)
candidates.effect: New/extended UW on the new line; candidate alongside the already-offered Göhl West.

candidates.name: Lübeck West
candidates.driver: Ostküstenleitung new substation (BBPlG 42)
candidates.effect: Brand-new UW near Lübeck; likely future connection point once the line energises (~2030/31).

candidates.name: Schwandorf
candidates.driver: Ostbayernring (BBPlG 18) + NEW NEP-2025 Zubau project P472 Schwandorf–Regensburg / Schwandorf–Wenzenbach
candidates.effect: Doubling the ring plus a brand-new 2025 line towards Regensburg; the 2036 Wenzenbach offer hangs on exactly this.

candidates.name: Bürstadt
candidates.driver: Rhein-Main-Link converters (BBPlG 82) + AMP-P159 Bürstadt–BASF (2029) + AMP-P310 Bürstadt–Kühmoos (2033)
candidates.effect: Up to 8 GW DC into the Rhine-Main data-centre belt (~2033/34); the structural answer to Frankfurt's load queue.

candidates.name: Emden/West
candidates.driver: New UW Suchraum Emden/West (NEP 2025), COD ~2034
candidates.effect: Second Emden node once A-Nord exports are running; load-only (electrolysis) logic.

candidates.name: Southern BW flips (Herbertingen · Grünkraut · Dellmensingen)
candidates.driver: AMP-P52/TNG-P52 Netzverstärkung südliches Baden-Württemberg, COD 2035 — runs through all three red nodes
candidates.effect: The 2035 corridor is the plausible un-redding of Oberschwaben; same pattern as the Rheinschiene nodes in cycle 1.

candidates.name: Kupferzell / Hüffenhardt
candidates.driver: P48 Grafenrheinfeld–Kupferzell done 2026; TNG-P430 rotating condensers with 1.25-GWs storage by 2027 (system strength)
candidates.effect: Red today, but system-strength hardware arriving is the Streumen pattern — offers often follow the stiffened grid.

candidates.name: Hanekenfähr–Gronau corridor
candidates.driver: AMP-P315 (BBPlG 63), COD 2034 — the project that already produced the Flör offer (phase-shifter)
candidates.effect: Emsland–Münsterland axis: more nodes along the new line as sections complete.

candidates.name: Raitersaich (Juraleitung)
candidates.driver: Juraleitung rebuild (BBPlG 41 / TTG-P53, construction from 2031) with NEW substations Raitersaich/West, Ludersheim/West, Sittling, Rottenburg (Punktmaßnahmen registry)
candidates.effect: Middle-Franconia corridor renewal creates four new switchgear sites in the second half of the 2030s.

tso_projects.name: UW Pulverdingen (Gesamtprojekt)
tso_projects.basis: website explicit (IBN Gesamtprojekt 2035; Neubau Bestandsanlage ab 2027)

tso_projects.name: UW Netze_West (Phasenschieber)
tso_projects.basis: estimated (NEP Zielnetz 2037/2045; website nennt kein Jahr)

analysis_method: All four NEP Projektsteckbriefe (2019, 2021, 2023, 2025 — 2,683 pages) and the NEP-2025 Punktmaßnahmen registry were machine-parsed (data/grid_reform/raw/nep/); per-node NEP trails (first appearance, project, COD) come from that index, cross-checked with BBPlG (netzausbau.de) and TSO project pages. Interpretations, not TSO statements.

capacity_semantics.requested: angefragt — pending applications in the queue; NOT yet granted. Measured against what is still available, not against the site maximum.
capacity_semantics.reserved: vergeben/reserviert — connection capacity already contractually committed, mostly granted first-come-first-served before the reform. It is gone.
capacity_semantics.available: verfügbar — what the TSO estimates it can still offer AFTER subtracting reservations (and its own needs) from the technical site limit. So: site maximum ≈ reserved + available.
capacity_semantics.oversubscription: requested ÷ available. Example Altentreptow Süd: 5,193 MW requested vs 650 MW still available (850 MW already reserved) — 8× oversubscribed.
capacity_semantics.caveat: The TSOs do not publish the technical site maximum itself; 'available' is their non-binding planning estimate per the 31.03.2026 map state.

tso_status.note: Per-substation queue: requested vs available MW, free bays and site BKZ (€/kW). Capacities for commissioning 2025–2029 exhausted; most sites connect only if a new bay can be built.
10 connection points presumed realisable, 2032–2035; bay design published, no MW bands.
10 substation projects with secured land and mature planning, connections 2030–2036; two of them load-only.
9 medium-term points from 2032 (green) and 6 long-term from 2035 (yellow), with indicative feed-in/load MW bands; 37 substations flagged 'nicht verfügbar' (red).
